visual_checks: Look for a link or section providing a full text transcript for prerecorded synchronized media
Check if the transcript includes descriptions of all visual information (actions, settings, expressions) in addition to dialogue
Verify the transcript is clearly associated with its corresponding media content
Check if the transcript includes speaker identification for dialogue
Look for descriptions of non-speech audio cues (music, sound effects) relevant to understanding the content
Verify the transcript is in a format accessible to assistive technologies (HTML text, not just a PDF or image)
pass_conditions: A full text alternative (descriptive transcript) is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media
The transcript includes all dialogue with speaker identification
The transcript includes descriptions of meaningful visual information (actions, settings, on-screen text, expressions)
Non-speech audio (sound effects, music) relevant to understanding is described in the transcript
The transcript is clearly linked to or associated with its media content
The transcript is in an accessible format (e.g., HTML text)
fail_conditions: Prerecorded synchronized media has no full text alternative or transcript
A transcript is provided but contains only dialogue without any visual descriptions
Visual information essential to understanding the content is missing from the transcript
The transcript is not clearly associated with its corresponding media
Speaker identification is missing, making dialogue attribution unclear
The transcript is in an inaccessible format (e.g., image of text, inaccessible PDF)
na_conditions: Page contains no prerecorded synchronized media
The media is live rather than prerecorded
Page contains only text content with no audio or video
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Media content and associated links failed to load so transcripts cannot be evaluated
common_mistakes: Providing captions but no full descriptive transcript that includes visual information
Transcript that only contains spoken dialogue without describing visual elements
Transcript placed on a separate page with no clear link from the media
Missing descriptions of on-screen text, charts, or demonstrations shown in the video
Providing a transcript as an image or scanned document instead of accessible text
Omitting non-speech audio cues that are important for understanding (e.g., alarm sounds, applause)

examples.pass: A recorded lecture video has a linked HTML transcript that includes all spoken content with speaker labels, descriptions of slides shown, on-screen demonstrations, and relevant sound cues
examples.fail: A product demo video has auto-generated captions but no full text transcript, and the captions do not describe any of the visual demonstrations
examples.partial: A transcript is provided for a training video that includes all dialogue but omits descriptions of the on-screen diagrams and visual demonstrations

plain_meaning: At Level AAA, every prerecorded synchronized media clip AND every prerecorded video-only clip must have a full text alternative for time-based media -- a document that describes all dialogue, all relevant non-speech audio, and all meaningful visual information in reading order. The intent is that a deafblind user reading with a braille display can reach the same content as a sighted hearing user.
scope_applies_to: All prerecorded synchronized media on sites claiming AAA conformance
All prerecorded video-only content on sites claiming AAA conformance
Embedded or self-hosted prerecorded media regardless of player
scope_does_not_apply_to: Prerecorded audio-only content -- covered by SC 1.2.1 at Level A
Audio description -- that belongs to SC 1.2.3 / 1.2.5
Sign language -- that belongs to SC 1.2.6
Keyboard operability of the player -- that belongs to SC 2.1.1
borderline_calibration: A linked transcript that covers both dialogue AND visuals satisfies the rule.
A captions-only approach is NOT a media alternative for time-based media; 1.2.8 demands a standalone text document.
This is AAA -- only evaluate when AAA is claimed or requested.
Missing text alternative on AAA-scope prerecorded media is a hard failure at medium+ within AAA context.
